title: Koeleria barrosii
taxonomy: Koeleria barrosii is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically belonging to the family Poaceae. This classification further breaks down into the order Poales, which is a group of monocotyledonous flowering plants. Within the family Poaceae, Koeleria barrosii is placed in the tribe Poeae. The tribe Poeae is a subfamily of the large Poaceae family, which includes various types of grasses. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Koeleria barrosii within the plant kingdom.
uses: Koeleria barrosii is a plant species native to South America, and its traditional uses extend to various fields, including medicine.  Although information is limited, it is used in folk medicine for its reported anti-inflammatory properties.  The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antioxidant and antimicrobial effects.  Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties of Koeleria barrosii.  Its traditional use in medicine suggests potential benefits, but more scientific studies are necessary for confirmation.
benefits: Koeleria barrosii is a type of grass, and its health benefits are not widely documented. preparations: Koeleria barrosii is a species of grass, and as such, there is limited information available on herbal preparations.
